# Forms are meant to become VARIANT ROWS in ingredients.csv
# (sprout makes=dal). A variant inherits the whole machine: stock
# the nightly prep queue
column2: the keep-a-batch-ready nag. This file is the GENERATOR SPEC for

column1: # those rows
column2: not a lookup you read by hand — so the numbers stay a formula and can be

column1: # regenerated when the formula improves.
# evidence = HOW WELL we know it. The flag that keeps an unquantified-but-real process usable
#   instead of silently absent:
#     quantified            a percentage exists and is cited. Safe to multiply.
#     sourced-unquantified  a named source states the DIRECTION but publishes no number. The
#                           planner may SURFACE it ("pressure-cooking helps here") and must NEVER
#                           compute with it. This is the state ICMR leaves pressure-cooking in.
#     gated                 real
column3: unquantified AND load-bearing enough that the

column1: #                           nutrient should not be credited at all until the form is applied
#                           (whole til's calcium). See `gates`.
#     unsourced             we believe it; nobody cited. Not actionable.
# mechanism = WHY absorption changes. This is the important column. `absorbed` is not one
#   calculation and the four families do not share a formula:
#   phytate  phytate chelates Fe/Zn/Ca -> molar ratio vs published thresholds     SOURCED
#   heat     nutrient destroyed by cooking -> see retention.csv (USDA RF6)        SOURCED
#   matrix   nutrient sealed inside an intact seed coat or cell wall              NOT SOURCED
#   fat      fat-soluble nutrient needs co-eaten fat to cross the gut wall        NOT SOURCED
# phyt_red = % phytate reduction. Only meaningful when mechanism=phytate.
#   BLANK means NOT MEASURED — do not read blank as zero and do not substitute a guess.
#   `raw` is the only genuine 0.
# gates = nutrients this form GATES rather than scales. A flag
column2: not a number: it means the

column1: #   listed nutrients should not be credited until the form is applied. Used where the effect
#   is real and directional but unquantified — whole til does deliver far less calcium than
#   ground til
column2: and refusing to invent the percentage is not the same as claiming no effect.

column1: #   The planner should WARN on a gated nutrient
# lead = HOURS of ahead-time the mode costs
# applies = ingredient ids or roles this mode is defined for. No commas in any field.
id

column9: ICMR: phytic acid in cereals+legumes 'decreases to a great extent' after boiling/pressure cooking making Fe Ca Mg Zn absorbable - DIRECTION sourced MAGNITUDE not

column1: boiled_drained
column9: ICMR: 'boiling with just enough water without having to drain is the best method to retain folate in legumes' - matches USDA RF6 numerically

column1: # CAVEATS - carried forward from nutrition-data-sources.md deliberately:
# 1. Only TWO of the four mechanisms above are sourced: phytate and heat. `matrix` and `fat`
#    carry gates instead of numbers. That is the honest state
column2: not an oversight to fill in later

column1: #    with a plausible-looking percentage.
# 2. The food-independence claim rests on THREE studies on THREE crops. Consistent but three.
# 3. Retention (what survives cooking) is NOT bioavailability (what gets absorbed). That axis
#    lives in retention.csv and MULTIPLIES with this one - it does not replace it. Legumes
#    boiled 45-75 min and DRAINED keep 45pc of folate and 70pc of potassium; keep the water
#    and it is 50pc and 75pc.
# 4. RDAs are expressed as CONSUMED not absorbed - they already divide by an assumed
#    bioavailability (WHO/FAO publishes 5pc 10pc and 15pc tiers). So absorbed figures must go
#    in SEPARATE columns. Rewriting ingredients.csv iron to absorbed would divide by
#    bioavailability twice and make every meal look short.
# 5. Nothing in this file is wired into app.py yet. It is a data layer waiting for a consumer.
